title: wrightia tinctoria subsp. rothii
intro: Wrightia tinctoria subsp. rothii, also known as the Indian root or black Wrightia, is a plant species belonging to the family Apocynaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to the Western Ghats region in India, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and managing diabetes, due to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids. Wrightia tinctoria subsp. rothii is often used in herbal preparations, including decoctions and infusions, as well as in the form of extracts and capsules, to harness its medicinal properties for various health issues.
